design_philosophy.core_loop: Master spinning sword combat with time-limited power-ups while navigating platforming challenges across seven themed rounds
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (bells are temporary) combined with platforming precision required to reach bosses
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as a radical ninja warrior on a quest to rescue a kidnapped princess, wielding legendary inherited weaponry
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Heavy reliance on a single signature weapon (spinning sword) with temporary power-up modifiers rather than weapon variety; straightforward rescue narrative framed in feudal Japan setting with 1980s 'radical' aesthetic (punk rock hair, attitude)

difficulty.lives_system: Standard NES lives system (specific count not documented)
difficulty.checkpointing: Round-based structure allows progression reset per round; no mid-round checkpoints documented
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Limited agency; fixed round structure and enemy placements with power-up collection as primary variable

progression.upgrade_system: Temporary power-ups via collectible bells: Golden Bell (projectile enhancement) and Silver Bell (defensive force field); both time-limited
progression.unlock_mechanism: Linear progression through seven sequential rounds, each concluding with a boss battle; no branching paths documented
replayability_hooks: Seven distinct themed rounds encourage multiple attempts to master patterns
Boss battles reward pattern recognition and memorization
Power-up timing and collection strategy varies per playthrough

world_building_techniques: Opening narrative setup via manual/instruction card (bird arrives with kidnapping note)
Feudal Japan setting frames ninja protagonist identity
Round structure implies geographical/thematic progression toward final villain confrontation
Character motivation (rescue girlfriend) provides throughline but minimal in-game narrative exposition

boss_design.philosophy: Seven themed boss encounters, one per round; suggests escalating challenge progression and thematic variety
notable_bosses: Stone Wizard (final antagonist who kidnapped Princess Margo)

danger_signals: Stone Wizard represents primary threat/evil force
Kidnapping of romantic interest signals high-stakes stakes
environmental_storytelling: Seven distinct round environments imply journey through varied territories
Feudal Japan aesthetic grounds the world
